ground_truth.title: Cybersecurity Best Practices | CalPERS
ground_truth.main_content:
We care about the security of our members and employers. In a lot of ways, your online safety better protects us from bad actors that may look to breach our systems. The following tips and recommendations are intended to help you stay safe online.

Secure Your Passwords: Our online passwords have become one of our more precious pieces of personal information — the stronger the password, the better protected you are from cybercriminals. Use a complex mix of letters, numbers, and, when allowed, symbols. Aim for a password length of at least 16 characters. Never reuse the same password for another site. Consider using a password manager on your computer or mobile device.

Limit Posting Identifiable Information Online: Whether on websites or social media applications, don't post any identifiable information that you wouldn't be comfortable with a stranger knowing. Assume any online activity will leave a permanent digital footprint.

Check Your Account Settings: Most online accounts have settings to help you keep your account secure. Multifactor Authentication (MFA) is a security process that requires more than one method of identity verification. Activity alerts initiate email, text, and/or other communications every time an account is logged in from a new device or browser. Personal security questions can provide another layer of security.

Email: Think Before You Click: If you receive an unanticipated email or an email from an unknown sender don't follow any links or open any attachments. Simply delete the email.

Mobile Device Precautions: With mobile devices, we increase our range of communications through phone calls, SMS/text, and other various messaging services. Messages, links, or attachments from unknown senders should be treated with care.

Unsecured Wi-Fi and Device Charging Stations: Public Wi-Fi signals are often unsecured and can lend your online details to spying third parties. Charging kiosks and public USB ports can be intentionally compromised by bad actors to steal information from any connected devices. Consider carrying your own external battery or use an AC adaptor.

Read Privacy Policies: A privacy policy is a declaration that describes how an organization will collect and use data about individuals who agree to it. If you don't have time to read it, don't accept it.

When to Contact Us: If you suspect your personally identifiable information or protected health information has been compromised in any way, contact us immediately by phone at 888 CalPERS (or 888-225-7377) or by secure message through myCalPERS.
